articles.title: Euclid Space Warps: help spot galaxies bending spacetime
A School of Mud Volcano Islands in Azerbaijan
articles.summary: The tadpole-shaped islands along the Absheron Peninsula were born by explosive mud volcano eruptions and reshaped by erosion.

articles.title: NASA IG Raises More Questions About Readiness for Human Lunar Landings
articles.summary: A new report from NASA’s Office of Inspector General raises more questions about whether landing humans on the Moon in 2028 is realistic. Last month the OIG pointed out that […]

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch final GPS III satellite for the U.S. Space Force
articles.summary: This is the final spacecraft of this generation of precision, navigation, and timing satellites before the U.S. Space Force shifts to launching GPS IIIF satellites. Liftoff from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled during a 15-minute launch window opens at 2:53 a.m. EDT (0653 UTC).

articles.title: NASA on Track for Future Missions with Initial Artemis II Assessments
articles.summary: Following NASA’s Artemis II mission successfully splashing down on Earth, engineers started diving into detailed analysis of data to assess how key systems and subsystems on the Orion spacecraft, SLS (Space Launch System) rocket, and systems at the launch pad at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida performed. articles.title: NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage
articles.summary: Following the recent successful test flight of NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon, NASA rolled out the core stage, or the largest section, of the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket that will launch the crewed Artemis III mission in 2027. The stage departed from the agency’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on […]

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to SpaceX’s 34th Resupply Launch to Space Station
articles.summary: Media accreditation is open for the next U.S. launch to deliver NASA science investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. This launch is the 34th SpaceX Commercial Resupply Services mission to the orbital laboratory for NASA and will lift off on the company’s Falcon 9 rocket. NASA and SpaceX are targeting no earlier […]

articles.title: Photos and Feelings from Artemis II
articles.summary: To commemorate Artemis II, Space Scout compiled photos and testimonials from our team members about this historic spaceflight.

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.summary: The Republic of Latvia signed the Artemis Accords Monday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s headquarters in Washington, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration for all humanity.  “We are proud to welcome Latvia to the Artemis Accords,” said NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. “Each new signatory strengthens a coalition committed to the transparent and peaceful exploration of space. The accords are the […]
